skill.name: run-tests
skill.description: Run unit tests, Node.js import checks, and guided integration tests for the Catalyst-Relay project.
skill.summary: Guides Catalyst-Relay test execution across unit, compatibility, and SAP integration workflows.
skill.author: Artisan-Edge

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell commands are mostly expected test commands documented in SKILL.md, not evidence of malware. The skill still has medium risk because it instructs users to run integration tests against a live SAP system with sensitive environment variables and a password argument. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Live SAP Integration Tests Use Sensitive Credentials
medium_findings.description: The integration workflow states that tests require SAP credentials and connect to a live SAP system. This is legitimate for this project, but it can expose credentials or affect live resources if used without approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly says integration tests require SAP credentials and a live SAP system. The context is a test workflow, so this is a real operational risk but not confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Password Passed as Command Argument
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks the user to run a test script with the SAP password as a command argument. Command-line passwords can be exposed through shell history, terminal logs, or process listings.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented command includes a password placeholder and the variable table confirms SAP_PASSWORD is required. The exposure risk is a standard concern for passwords passed on command lines.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Mostly Expected Test Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged shell command examples for running Bun tests, a Node.js import check, and a test batch file. These are normal for a test-running skill, but users should review commands before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation examples, not hidden execution logic. They still create normal command-execution risk if an agent runs them without user awareness.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography findings at the metadata description and table header do not show cryptographic algorithms or cryptographic operations. No evidence found for weak crypto usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain a prose description and a Markdown table header. There is no cryptographic code, algorithm name, or security function on those lines.

low_findings.title: Environment Template Reference Exposes Secret Names Only
low_findings.description: The skill references an environment template and lists required SAP variable names. This does not expose secret values, but it confirms that the workflow depends on sensitive configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows environment variable names and a template reference, not secret values. The listed names include credentials, so the operational concern is real but limited.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents commands that run tests and import checks. These should be treated as user-approved project commands, not automatically trusted marketplace behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and related to testing. The risk is contextual because command execution is expected for this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Environment Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The integration test workflow depends on SAP URL, client, username, password, package, and transport environment values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable table explicitly lists SAP credential and system configuration names. No secret values are present in the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Live Network Integration Testing
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill says integration tests connect to a live SAP system. This can have external effects outside the local development environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file directly states that integration tests connect to a live SAP system and requires a SAP ADT server URL. This confirms external network dependency.

content.value_statement: Catalyst-Relay contributors need a clear path for unit, compatibility, and integration testing. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the documented test workflow.
actual_capabilities: Lists when to run tests during Catalyst-Relay development.
Provides Bun commands for all tests, watch mode, and targeted directories.
Provides a Node.js import compatibility check before publishing.
Describes the SAP integration test workflow and required variables.
Maps integration test files to the workflows they cover.
limitations: It is specific to the Catalyst-Relay project structure.
Integration tests require SAP credentials and a live SAP system.
It does not validate credentials or provision test environments.
It relies on existing project scripts and test files.

use_cases.title: Verify a Feature Change
use_cases.description: Run the relevant Bun test command after changing Catalyst-Relay code.

use_cases.title: Check Release Readiness
use_cases.description: Run full tests and the Node.js import check before publishing.

use_cases.title: Coordinate SAP Integration Testing
use_cases.description: Prepare required SAP variables and review integration test results.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-tests skill to run the standard Catalyst-Relay unit tests and summarize the result.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation after a small code change

prompt_templates.title: Run Targeted Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-tests skill to identify and run the most relevant tests for my recent Catalyst-Relay change.

prompt_templates.title: Check Before Publish
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-tests skill to run release verification, including the Node.js import compatibility check.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Integration Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-tests skill to prepare the SAP integration test workflow, confirm required variables, and review test.output when ready.

output_examples.input: Run the standard tests for this Catalyst-Relay change.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs the documented Bun test command, reports pass or fail status, and highlights any failing test names.

output_examples.input: Check whether this is ready to publish.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs unit tests and the Node.js import check, then summarizes remaining release blockers.

output_examples.input: Help me run integration tests with SAP.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists required SAP variables, asks for user confirmation before live testing, and reviews the saved test output.

best_practices: Run unit tests before integration tests to catch local failures first.
Confirm live SAP test access with the user before running integration workflows.
Review test.output after integration testing and summarize only needed details.
anti_patterns: Do not run live SAP integration tests without explicit user approval.
Do not print or store SAP passwords in conversation summaries.
Do not treat the Node.js compatibility check as a replacement for full tests.

faq.question: What project is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is written for the Catalyst-Relay repository and its test workflow.

faq.question: Can it run all unit tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents the Bun command for running the full unit test suite.

faq.question: Does it support targeted tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an example for running tests in a specific directory.

faq.question: Why is there a medium security risk?
faq.answer: The integration workflow uses live SAP access and sensitive credentials.

faq.question: Does the skill contain secrets?
faq.answer: No evidence found. It lists variable names but not secret values.

faq.question: Should integration tests run automatically?
faq.answer: No. They should run only after the user confirms credentials and live-system access.
